parsed.production_notes: The video features a real human actor in practical, real-world locations like a home and a car. Lighting, camera movement, and physical interactions are natural and consistent with standard live-action social media content.

characters.appearance: Young Asian woman, slim build, fair complexion. Long dark hair, usually tied back. Expressive and energetic facial features.
characters.wardrobe: Varies: blue striped shirt, grey ribbed top, black long-sleeve with track pants, dark green t-shirt. Casual and comfortable styling.

characters.appearance: Not visible; represented by the app interface and voice.

scenes.setting: Various: desk, car, living room. Mix of indoor lamp and natural daylight.

panels.panel_visual_description: A montage shows Character A working at a desk, driving a car, and walking through a living room. She then collapses onto a white sofa, holding her smartphone. Lighting varies from indoor lamps to natural daylight.

scenes.visual_description: A fast-paced montage establishes Character A's busy lifestyle across different locations. Text overlays emphasize her lack of time and desire to learn English. The scene ends with her exhausted on the couch, introducing the app.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: 英語習得中の社会人です。 [I am a working professional currently learning English.]
Character A says: 日本に住んでて仕事も忙しい。 [I live in Japan and am busy with work.]
Character A says: 英語を使う環境もほぼないけど、英語喋れるようになりてぇ。 [I have almost no environment to use English, but I wanna be able to speak it.]
Character A says: 結局喋りまくるしかねぇ。 [In the end, I just have to speak a lot.]
scenes.on_screen_text:
英語習得中の社会人です (I am a working professional currently learning English)
日本に住んでて仕事も忙しい (I live in Japan and am busy with work)
英語を使う環境もほぼないけど (I have almost no environment to use English, but)
英語喋れるようになりてぇぇぇぇ (I wanna be able to speak English)
結局喋りまくるしかねェ (In the end, I just have to speak a lot)
だから今私 (So now, I)

scenes.setting: Modern kitchen with green tiled walls. Bright indoor lighting.

panels.panel_visual_description: Character A stands in a kitchen with green tiled walls, wearing a black top and track pants. She chops a tomato on a cutting board. A smartphone rests on an orange stand nearby.

scenes.visual_description: Character A cooks in her kitchen while using the language app hands-free. The app interface is overlaid on the left side of the screen, showing the AI conversation. Text overlays highlight the convenience of practicing while doing chores.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: だから今私、毎日Fluentlyで英語スピーキングやってる。 [So now, I practice English speaking every day with Fluently.]
Character B says: Yeah! So you'd say, "I came across a really good recipe on YouTube Shorts." Nice and natural. By the way, that combo - Mexican salsa and Chinese stir-fry - sounds wild! What made you pick those two together? [Yeah! So you'd say, "I came across a really good recipe on YouTube Shorts." Nice and natural. By the way, that combo - Mexican salsa and Chinese stir-fry - sounds wild! What made you pick those two together?]
Character A says: Just... I'm craving for. [Just... I'm craving for.]
Character B says: Use "craving" alone when expressing what you want. [Use "craving" alone when expressing what you want.]
scenes.on_screen_text:
毎日 Fluently で 英語スピーキング (Every day, English speaking with Fluently)
ハンズフリーで使えるから料理中にやってる。 (I use it hands-free, so I do it while cooking.)
That combo- Mexican salsa and Chinese stirfry sounds wild! (That combo- Mexican salsa and Chinese stirfry sounds wild!)
メキシカンと中華の組み合わせ なんか (Mexican and Chinese combination, somehow)
Just... I'm craving for. (Just... I'm craving for.)
食べたいんだもん。 (I just want to eat it.)
すぐに間違いを直してくれる！ (It corrects mistakes immediately!)
Use "craving" alone when expressing what you want (Use "craving" alone when expressing what you want)

scenes.setting: Living room and indoor seating area. Warm indoor lighting.

panels.panel_visual_description: Character A, in a green t-shirt, stands in a living room holding her phone and speaking enthusiastically. The shot cuts to her sitting indoors, holding the phone and looking focused.

scenes.visual_description: Character A interacts with the app, simulating a natural phone call. The app interface is shown on the phone screen, displaying the conversation and corrections. Text overlays emphasize the realistic feel of the AI tutor.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: まじでネイティブと電話してるみたいにAIと喋れる！ [I can seriously talk with AI like I'm on a phone call with a native speaker!]
Character A says: 通話してる感覚、そのまんまだから、料理しながらでも自然に英語出てくる。 [It feels exactly like a phone call, so English comes out naturally even while cooking.]
Character A says: I make... not too close, not too far boundaries between students and teachers. [I make... not too close, not too far boundaries between students and teachers.]
Character B says: When talking about teacher, student relationships, "set boundaries" is a more natural phrase. That sounds really professional. How do you usually set that boundary with your students? [When talking about teacher, student relationships, "set boundaries" is a more natural phrase. That sounds really professional. How do you usually set that boundary with your students?]
scenes.on_screen_text:
まじでネイティブと電話してるみたいにAIと喋れる！ (I can seriously talk with AI like I'm on a phone call with a native speaker!)
"通話してる感覚" そのまんまだから (It feels exactly like a phone call, so)
料理しながらでも自然に英語出てくる (English comes out naturally even while cooking)
仕事の話で盛り上がってる (We are having a lively conversation about work)
I make... 私は (I make... I)
Not too close, not too far boundaries 近すぎない、遠すぎない距離を (Not too close, not too far boundaries)
between students and teachers. 生徒と講師との間で意識してるかな。 (between students and teachers. I am conscious of it between students and instructors.)
When talking about 生徒と講師の (When talking about student and instructor's)
teacher, student relationships (teacher, student relationships)
"Set boundaries" (Set boundaries)
gaps set boundaries between students and teachers is more natural phrase. こっちの方がナチュラルだよ。 (gaps set boundaries between students and teachers is more natural phrase. This one is more natural.)
That sounds really professional プロっぽいねぇ (That sounds really professional. Sounds professional.)
How do you usually set that boundary with your students? 生徒との距離を保ってるの？ (How do you usually set that boundary with your students? Do you keep a distance from your students?)

scenes.setting: Desk area and spacious room with a mirror. Indoor lighting.

panels.panel_visual_description: A hand holds a smartphone. Character A sits at a desk with a tablet and phone. She then paces in a spacious room with a large mirror, holding her phone and talking.

scenes.visual_description: The scene highlights the app's real-time correction feature. Character A is shown deeply engaged in a long conversation while moving around her home. Text overlays explain how the app corrects mistakes without interrupting the flow.
scenes.audio_transcript: Character A says: ほらぁ、間違えた瞬間リアルタイムで直してくれて、会話の流れ妨げずにそのまんま続けられるから、あっという間に1時間喋り倒してた。 [See, the moment I make a mistake, it corrects me in real-time, and I can just keep going without interrupting the flow of the conversation, so before I knew it, I had been talking non-stop for an hour.]
scenes.on_screen_text:
ほらぁ (See)
間違えた瞬間リアルタイムで直してくれて (The moment I make a mistake, it corrects me in real-time)
会話の流れ妨げずにそのまんま続けられるから (I can just keep going without interrupting the flow of the conversation, so)
あっという間に1時間喋り倒してた (Before I knew it, I had been talking non-stop for an hour)
マジで一番良くて (Seriously the best part)

panels.panel_visual_description: Close-ups show a hand holding a smartphone. Character A, in a green t-shirt, smiles and speaks to the camera. She is briefly seen cooking again, then sits at a table holding the phone towards the viewer.

scenes.visual_description: Character A reviews her mistakes using the app's features. The screen shows vocabulary and pronunciation exercises. She concludes by encouraging viewers to download the app, with a final call-to-action overlay.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: で、ここがマジで一番良くて。 [And this is seriously the best part.]
Character A says: 自分が間違えた単語だけ集中して練習できるの。 [I can focus and practice only the words I got wrong.]
Character A says: めっちゃ効率いいよね。 [It's super efficient, right?]
Character A says: fragile [fragile]
Character B says: fragile [fragile]
Character A says: 終わったら弱点だけ復習。 [When I'm done, I just review my weak points.]
Character A says: これ毎日やってたら絶対伸びるやつじゃん。 [If I do this every day, I'll definitely improve.]
Character A says: みんなもこれで英語勉強一緒にやろうぜ！ [Let's all study English together with this!]
scenes.on_screen_text:
間違えた単語だけ集中して練習できるの (I can focus and practice only the words I got wrong)
めっちゃ効率いいよね (It's super efficient, right?)
発音が誤っていた単語だけピンポイントで練習できる (You can pinpoint and practice only the words you mispronounced)
fragile (fragile)
弱点だけ復習 (Review only weak points)
個人的に"ながら使い"しやすいのが気に入ってる (Personally, I like that it's easy to use while doing other things)
絶対伸びるやつじゃん (It's definitely something that will make you improve)
みんなもFluentlyで英語勉強一緒にやろうぜ！ (Let's all study English together with Fluently!)
